parts.text: Show me an image of the brake Wear indicator

Based on the Bombardier Challenger 605 Landing Gear manual, the **Brake Wear Indicator** consists of two indicator pins installed on each brake assembly to provide a visual reference of the brake's condition.

### **Description of the Brake Wear Indicator**
*   **Location:** There are two brake wear indicator pins on each brake assembly (on the main landing gear).
*   **Visual Indication:** The brakes are considered worn and should be serviced when the end of the **wear indicator pin is flush with the top of the indicator housing**.
*   **Check Procedure:** To get an accurate reading, the brake wear indicator pins must be checked while the **brakes are applied** and **hydraulic systems 2 and 3 are pressurized**.

### **Reference Image Information**
You can find the visual representation of the brakes and the associated structures in the following figure:
*   **Figure Number:** Figure 15-10-8
*   **Title:** Brakes and Lower Main Gear Structure
*   **Document:** CL605-LANDING_GEAR.pdf
*   **Page Number:** 15 (Note: The text description starts on Page 14 under the "Wheels and Brakes" section).

**Note:** If the pin is no longer protruding from the housing while the brakes are pressurized, it indicates that the carbon brake discs have worn down to their limit and maintenance is required.

root_agent.name: root_agent
root_agent.instructions:
You are an expert assistant for Bombardier, helping users query airplane plans and user manuals.
Your goal is to provide helpful, accurate answers based ONLY on the provided documents.
When answering:
1. If the user asks about an image, diagram, or figure (or a part in them), you MUST search for it and describe it.
2. You should mention the figure number (e.g., Figure 15-10-9) or page number where the information or image is found.
3. Provide a clear reference to the document and page for proof.
4. Be very helpful and precise in your instructions (e.g., for procedures like turning on the parking brake).
5. If you cannot find the information in the documents, state that you cannot find it. Do not make up information.

You are an agent. Your internal name is "root_agent".

Show me an image of the brake Wear indicator

Based on the Bombardier Challenger 605 Landing Gear manual, the **Brake Wear Indicator** consists of two indicator pins installed on each brake assembly to provide a visual reference of the brake's condition.

### **Description of the Brake Wear Indicator**

*   **Check Procedure:** To get an accurate reading, the brake wear indicator pins must be checked while the **brakes are applied** and **hydraulic systems 2 and 3 are pressurized**.

### **Reference Image Information**
You can find the visual representation of the brakes and the associated structures in the following figure:
*   **Figure Number:** Figure 15-10-8
*   **Title:** Brakes and Lower Main Gear Structure
*   **Document:** CL605-LANDING_GEAR.pdf
*   **Page Number:** 15 (Note: The text description starts on Page 14 under the "Wheels and Brakes" section).

retrieved_context.text:
WHEELS AND BRAKES (CONT'D) PARKING BRAKE Handle
Figure 15−10−10 Anti-Skid System
The anti-skid system consists of a dual-channel (inboard and outboard) anti-skid control unit
(ASCU), four axle-mounted speed transducers, and four anti-skid control valves. The anti-skid
system independently controls the braking of each main wheel, by modulating the hydraulic
pressure applied to each brake. Rotational speed of each wheel and its rate of deceleration are sensed by the wheel speed
transducers, and the data is transmitted to the ASCU. The ASCU modulates the hydraulic
pressure at the brake to prevent wheel lockup. Comparing the speed of paired wheels provides a second level of anti-skid protection. The
inboard wheels form one pair, the outboard wheels the other. If, for example, the right inboard
wheel rotates at a significantly slower speed than the left inboard, the ASCU releases the brake
on the slower turning wheel. When both paired wheels return to the same speed, the ASCU
reapplies the released brake. The ARMED switch on the ANTI-SKID control panel arms the system when the parking brake is
not engaged, and both MLG are down and locked. In air mode (no WOW signal), the ASCU
configures the anti-skid valves to full brake dump, to prevent wheel lockup at touchdown. 
...
The
system becomes operational when a 35-knot wheel spin-up signal is generated, or a W0W
signal is present after a 5-second delay. The anti-skid system is functional down to a minimum
wheel spin of 10 knots. Anti-Skid Test
The TEST button on the LDG GEAR control panel initiates a built-in test of the ASCU and
associated components, during which time the A/SKID IN TEST advisory EICAS message will
be displayed. The test can only be performed with the parking brake off. Bombardier Challenger 605 - Landing Gear Page 16
retrieved_context.title: CL605-LANDING_GEAR
